localization_scope: admitted runtime procedures in the active selector/runtime chain using existing packet and code evidence only

command_surfaces: status --json delegated health path when recovery is needed
evidence_basis: the helper itself holds serialized_lock across subprocess.run([launcher_script, smoke])
the bounded retry contour showed the same live holder pid 53546 on sync and on immediate rotation-after-sync, which fits a longer critical section better than a short observation lock
status delegates to healthcheck by default, so the shared helper remains reachable from admitted runtime observation surfaces even when launch smoke is not the explicit current contour

the prior owner-path contour identified the launcher-backed owner lane as the strongest class; this contour tightens that class to the shared helper procedure reused by both healthcheck/status recovery and launch smoke
counterpoints: no packet in the current artifact chain identifies which exact top-level command invoked this helper while pid 53546 held the lock
status_after_sync later reported deterministic_stable_recovery_result.status=not_invoked, so the observed post-holder status packet does not itself prove that recovery path was the holder

command_surfaces: status --json delegated health path when proxy-path auto-adoption is triggered
evidence_basis: this helper also holds serialized_lock around subprocess activation and is reachable from healthcheck/status owner paths
counterpoints: current packet chain does not carry proxy-path-broken evidence around the repeated selector failure
the active retry chain gives weaker support to proxy adoption than to stable launcher recovery mechanics

evidence_basis: rotation inspect shares the same lock and was actually executed immediately before and after sync in the bounded retry contour
counterpoints: its critical section is short and the same-holder-pid symptom across two blocked commands is a weaker fit for this procedure than for a launcher-backed helper

evidence_basis: a second sync process would block the first on the same lock
counterpoints: the current packet chain does not show a second sync command in flight
this procedure explains the lock symptom only generically and does not tighten localization

improvement_basis: the prior contour stopped at a top-level launcher-lane family
this contour localizes the highest-probability overlap to the shared helper procedure reused by multiple admitted command surfaces

packet_basis.fact: the bounded retry contour itself ran rotation inspect, sync, rotation inspect, then status
sync returned LOCK_HELD with holder pid 53546 and changed_files=[]
immediate post-sync rotation inspect hit the same holder pid 53546
status delegates to healthcheck and later observed deterministic_stable_recovery_result.status=not_invoked after the holder was gone
launch smoke belonged to the earlier runtime reproof contour and re-earned runtime green before the later selector retry contour

code_basis.fact: run_stable_runtime_launcher_attempt holds serialized_lock across the smoke subprocess
summarize_status delegates into run_healthcheck by default
healthcheck invokes run_stable_runtime_launcher_attempt when stable listener recovery is entered
launch smoke invokes the same shared helper directly
rollout rotation inspect also shares the same lock but only for an observation critical section
